pattern: The conversions form a rooted tree from unit 0, so a single traversal multiplies edge factors along each unique path under a modulus.
hints: Each unit (except 0) is reached by exactly one chain of conversions, so the factors describe a tree rooted at unit 0.
If you know how many base-0 units equal one unit of the source, you get the target by multiplying that running product by the edge factor.
Build an adjacency list of (target, factor) edges, then BFS or DFS from unit 0 carrying the accumulated product modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: branching tree where order of edges is not the order of units
factors compound large enough to require the modulus
factor of one keeps the product unchanged down a deep chain
star graph: every unit reached directly from 0

stress.description: random tree on up to size+1 units with each new node attached to an earlier one - exercises deep paths and the modulus
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    conv = []
    for v in range(1, n):
        u = rng.randrange(0, v)
        f = rng.randrange(1, 10**9 + 1)
        conv.append([u, v, f])
    rng.shuffle(conv)
    return [conv]
stress.note: edges shuffled so traversal order differs from unit order
